Lights Out
Turn off all the lights! Clicking a light toggles it and its neighbors. Find the pattern to extinguish every light on the grid. Simple but challenging!

Lights Out is the classic puzzle game that challenges your logical thinking! A grid of lights is randomly illuminated, and clicking any light toggles it along with its four neighbors (up, down, left, right). Your goal is simple: turn off ALL the lights. Is there always a solution?
Yes! Every Lights Out puzzle has exactly one solution. In fact, there's a mathematical method called the 'light-chasing' technique that can solve any puzzle systematically.
Use 'light chasing': Work from top to bottom. For each row, click the cells below any lights that are on. This 'chases' all lights to the bottom row, which you then solve with the correct pattern.
Does clicking a cell twice help?
No! Clicking a cell twice returns it to its original state, making your second click wasted. An efficient solution never clicks the same cell more than once.
Is Lights Out mathematical?

Yes! Lights Out is essentially linear algebra over the field GF(2). Each cell is a variable, each click is an equation. The solution is found through matrix operations—though humans solve it intuitively!

How is this different from Mahjong Solitaire?

Mahjong Titans focuses on memory rather than accessibility. All tiles start face-down, and you must remember positions. There's also a time limit, making it more fast-paced than the relaxed layered version.
What's the best memory strategy?

Focus on one area at a time, use mnemonic devices to remember positions, and try to recall patterns rather than individual tiles. Some players prefer to reveal tiles systematically rather than randomly.
What happens when time runs out?
The game ends with your current score. Any remaining tiles that weren't matched count against your final score. Speed and accuracy both matter!
Does playing improve memory?
Yes! The game exercises working memory and spatial recall. Regular play can help improve your ability to remember positions and visual patterns.

Mastermind is the classic code-breaking board game that has challenged logical thinkers since 1970! Your mission is to crack a secret code of colored pegs using deduction and strategic thinking. After each guess, feedback pegs reveal how close you are—black pegs mean correct color in correct position, white pegs mean correct color in wrong position. Can colors repeat in the code?
Yes! The secret code can contain the same color multiple times. For example, RED-RED-BLUE-GREEN is a valid code.
What's the best first guess?
Many players use 4 different colors (like RED-BLUE-GREEN-YELLOW) to maximize information from the first guess feedback.
How do I interpret the white pegs?
White pegs tell you that a color is in the code but in the wrong position. Use process of elimination to figure out which color goes where.
Is Mastermind always solvable in 8 moves?
With optimal play, yes! Mathematical analysis shows that any code can be cracked in 5 or fewer guesses with perfect strategy.

Masyu
Draw a loop passing through all pearls! White pearls require a straight path, black pearls require a turn. No guessing needed - pure logic!

Masyu is the elegant Japanese logic puzzle that tests your spatial reasoning! Draw a single continuous loop that passes through every pearl on the grid. White pearls require the loop to pass straight through, while black pearls require a turn. This pearl placement creates logical constraints that make every puzzle solvable without guessing—pure deduction from start to finish!

Draw a single continuous loop that passes through every pearl. At white pearls, the loop must go straight through (no turn). At black pearls, the loop must turn. The loop cannot cross itself or branch.
Click on edges between dots to draw line segments. The loop must pass through all pearls. At white pearls, go straight through. At black pearls, make a turn.
Click on the edges between grid dots to draw line segments. Click again to remove a segment. Build the loop section by section until it forms a complete closed path.

Look for pearls near edges - they constrain the loop

What do white pearls require?

White pearls require the loop to pass straight through them—the loop must enter and exit in opposite directions (no turn at the pearl). However, the loop CAN turn immediately before or after the white pearl.
What do black pearls require?
Black pearls require the loop to make a turn at that exact position. The loop must enter and exit at a 90-degree angle, forming a corner at the black pearl.
Can the loop cross itself?
No! The loop must be a single continuous path that never crosses itself or branches. It's one closed curve that visits every pearl exactly once.
Is guessing ever needed?
No! Well-designed Masyu puzzles can be solved entirely through logical deduction. If you're guessing, look harder for the logical constraints you may have missed.

Match 4 gems creates a line-clearing gem. Match 5 creates a rainbow gem that clears all gems of one color. Special combinations have unique effects!
How do combos work?
When gems fall and create new matches automatically, each consecutive match in the chain adds a score multiplier. Longer chains = bigger bonuses!
Focus on creating special gems rather than basic 3-matches. Look for opportunities to set up chain reactions. Bottom-of-board matches often trigger cascades.
Can I swap any two gems?
Only adjacent gems can be swapped, and the swap must create at least one match of 3 or more. Invalid swaps are rejected.

Memory Grid challenges you to remember and repeat increasingly long sequences of highlighted cells! Watch carefully as cells flash in a specific order, then click them in the exact same sequence. How many steps can most people remember?
The average person can remember 5-9 items in sequence. With practice, many reach 10-15. Memory champions can handle 20+ through special techniques.
What's the best way to remember sequences?
Create a story or path connecting the cells. Some people verbalize positions ('top-left, center, bottom-right'). Chunking—grouping cells into patterns—also helps.
Does order really matter?

Is this different from Pattern Memory?
Yes. Pattern Memory tests remembering a static visual arrangement. Memory Grid tests remembering a temporal sequence—what happened in what order.

Memory
Classic card-matching game! Flip cards to find matching pairs. Train your visual memory and concentration. Great for all ages!

Memory is the beloved card-matching game that has challenged minds for generations! Cards are laid face-down in a grid, each hiding an image that has a matching twin. Flip two cards at a time to find pairs—if they match, they stay revealed; if not, they flip back. This simple yet engaging gameplay trains visual memory, pattern recognition, and concentration. Perfect for players of all ages, from young children developing memory skills to adults keeping their minds sharp!
Find all matching pairs of cards by remembering their positions. Complete the game with as few moves as possible to achieve a high score. Train your visual memory and spatial recall while having fun!
Click cards to flip them. Find all matching pairs to win. The fewer moves you make, the better your score. Challenge yourself to remember card positions!

Practice regularly to improve memory skills
How can I improve my Memory game performance?
Practice regularly, create mental associations for card positions, use chunking (grouping nearby cards), and stay focused. Naming cards aloud can also help memory retention.
What's considered a good score?
For a standard 16-card (8 pair) game, finding all pairs in 10-12 moves is excellent. 8-9 moves is near-perfect. For larger grids, efficiency percentage is a better metric.
Does playing Memory help brain function?
Yes! Memory games exercise working memory, visual processing, and concentration. Regular play can help maintain cognitive function and may improve short-term memory capacity.
Many players use a systematic approach, starting from one corner and working across. Others prefer to quickly reveal many cards first, then focus on matches. Find what works for your memory style!

src/content/games/minesweeper.json
nim
Nim
An ancient mathematical strategy game! Take stones from piles, but take the last stone and you lose. Perfect information, pure strategy - can you find the winning formula?

Nim is one of the oldest mathematical games in history, dating back centuries! Multiple piles of stones sit before you—take any number from one pile each turn. In the standard version, the player who takes the last stone loses. Despite simple rules, Nim contains deep mathematical theory using binary XOR operations. Perfect information, no luck—pure strategic thinking!

Force your opponent to take the last stone (or be the one to take it in misère version). Plan your moves to leave your opponent in losing positions. Master the mathematical theory for guaranteed wins against suboptimal play!
Select a row, then choose how many stones to remove. You must take at least 1 stone. The player forced to take the last stone loses the game.
Click on a pile to select it, then choose how many stones to remove. You can take any number from 1 to all stones in that pile. Players alternate turns until someone takes the last stone.
Take 1 or more stones from one pile
Cannot take from multiple piles
Player taking last stone loses
No hidden information
Perfect play is calculable
Pure Strategy - No luck involved
Mathematical Theory - XOR-based solution
Multiple Difficulties - Easy to hard AI
Educational - Learn game theory
In Hard mode, the AI uses optimal XOR strategy
Try to leave your opponent with equal rows
Control the total stone count
Learn the mathematical theory for perfect play
What's the XOR strategy in Nim?

Calculate the XOR (exclusive or) of all pile sizes in binary. If the result is 0, you're in a losing position with perfect play. If non-zero, there's always a move to put your opponent in a losing position.
Is Nim always winnable for one player?
Yes! With perfect play, the winner is determined by the starting position. If the initial XOR is 0, the second player wins. If non-zero, the first player wins. This is mathematically proven!
What's misère Nim?
Misère is the standard version where taking the last stone loses. Normal play (taking last wins) is actually easier to analyze. The strategies differ slightly near the endgame.
How old is the game of Nim?
Nim's origins are unclear but it's certainly centuries old, possibly ancient Chinese. It was studied mathematically in 1901 by Charles Bouton, who discovered the XOR winning strategy.
尼姆博弈
古老的数学策略游戏！从石堆中取走石头，但取走最后一颗石头就输。完全信息，纯粹策略 - 你能找到必胜公式吗？
尼姆博弈是历史上最古老的数学游戏之一，可以追溯到几个世纪前！多堆石头摆在你面前——每轮从一堆中取任意数量。在标准版本中，取走最后一颗石头的玩家输。尽管规则简单，尼姆包含使用二进制XOR运算的深刻数学理论。完全信息，没有运气——纯粹的策略思考！
迫使你的对手取走最后一颗石头（或者在misère版本中成为取走它的人）。规划你的移动使对手处于必败位置。掌握数学理论以在次优对局中保证获胜！
点击堆选择要拿取的物品数。每轮必须至少拿取一个，最多拿取堆中剩余物品数。谁拿走最后一个物品谁获胜。
点击一堆选择它，然后选择要移除多少颗石头。你可以从该堆取1到所有石头的任何数量。玩家轮流直到有人取走最后一颗石头。

尼姆博弈中的XOR策略是什么？
计算所有堆大小的二进制XOR（异或）。如果结果是0，你在完美发挥下处于必败位置。如果非零，总有一个移动可以将对手置于必败位置。
尼姆博弈对一方来说总是可赢的吗？
是的！在完美发挥下，获胜者由起始位置决定。如果初始XOR是0，后手获胜。如果非零，先手获胜。这是数学证明的！
什么是misère尼姆？
Misère是标准版本，取走最后一颗石头输。正常玩法（取最后赢）实际上更容易分析。策略在终局略有不同。
尼姆博弈有多古老？
尼姆博弈的起源不清楚，但肯定有几个世纪的历史，可能来自中国古代。它在1901年由Charles Bouton进行数学研究，他发现了XOR获胜策略。

The average person can remember 7±2 digits. Reaching 9-10 digits is above average. Exceptional memory athletes can recall 20+ digits with training.
How can I remember more digits?
Use chunking (group into 2-3 digit segments), create mental associations, and practice regularly. Some people find it helpful to say the numbers aloud.
What is working memory?
Working memory is your brain's 'scratchpad'—it temporarily holds information you're actively using. It's different from long-term memory and can be improved with practice.
Why do numbers disappear so quickly?
The brief display tests your working memory capacity. Longer display times would allow you to use other strategies like rehearsal, which isn't what this test measures.

Nurikabe
Build walls to form islands! Numbered cells indicate island size. Islands contain exactly one number and never touch each other. Pure logic puzzle!

Nurikabe is the Japanese logic puzzle that challenges you to build an archipelago! Each numbered cell is an island, and the number shows how many cells that island occupies. Surround these islands with a continuous ocean of black cells (walls). Islands can never touch each other, all water must connect, and no 2×2 black areas are allowed. A perfect blend of spatial reasoning and logical deduction!

Shade cells to create a continuous ocean (black cells) surrounding numbered islands. Each numbered cell shows its island's size. Islands must not touch each other. All black cells must connect. No 2×2 blocks of black cells allowed!
Click cells to toggle between empty (gray), black (wall), or white (island). Each number shows its island's size. Islands can't touch. All walls must connect. No 2x2 black blocks.

What's the key Nurikabe strategy?

Start with 1s—they must be isolated, surrounded by black. Then expand from each number, remembering that islands can't touch. Always keep the ocean (black cells) connected and avoid 2×2 black squares.
Why no 2×2 black blocks?
The 2×2 rule prevents simply filling everything with black. It forces you to create interesting island shapes and ensures puzzles have unique solutions. It's a key constraint for logical deduction.
How do I know if black cells are connected?
You should be able to trace a path through black cells from any black cell to any other without crossing white islands. If you create isolated black areas, the puzzle is unsolvable.
Can islands touch diagonally?
Yes! Islands cannot share an edge (orthogonal contact), but diagonal touching is fine. This is an important distinction that allows more complex island shapes.

从1开始——它们必须被孤立，被黑色包围。然后从每个数字扩展，记住岛屿不能接触。始终保持海洋（黑色单元格）连接，避免2×2黑色方块。
为什么没有2×2黑色块？
2×2规则防止简单地用黑色填充所有内容。它强迫你创建有趣的岛屿形状，确保谜题有独特解决方案。这是逻辑推理的关键约束。

你应该能够通过黑色单元格从任何黑色单元格追踪路径到任何其他，而不穿过白色岛屿。如果你创建了孤立的黑色区域，谜题就无法解决。

Pac-Man
Eat all dots while avoiding colorful ghosts

Pac-Man is the most iconic video game character in history! Navigate through a maze filled with dots while being chased by four colorful ghosts—Blinky, Pinky, Inky, and Clyde. Eat all the dots to advance to the next level, but be careful: one touch from a ghost costs a life! Power pellets turn the tables, allowing you to eat the ghosts for bonus points. This 1980 classic defined the maze chase genre and remains as addictive today as ever.

Clear each maze by eating all the dots while avoiding or hunting ghosts. Maximize your score by eating ghosts during power-up mode and collecting bonus fruits. Survive as many levels as possible as the ghosts become faster and more aggressive.
Use arrow keys to guide Pac-Man through the maze. Eat all dots to win. Avoid ghosts unless powered up. Power pellets make ghosts vulnerable temporarily.

What do the different ghosts do?
Blinky (red) chases directly, Pinky (pink) ambushes from ahead, Inky (cyan) uses complex targeting, and Clyde (orange) has random behavior when close.
How long do power pellets last?
Power pellets make ghosts vulnerable for a limited time that decreases as you advance levels. Eat ghosts quickly before they turn back!
What's the maximum score from eating ghosts?
Eating all four ghosts after one power pellet scores 200+400+800+1600=3000 points. The sequence resets with each power pellet.
Why use the tunnels?
Pac-Man maintains normal speed through tunnels while ghosts slow down, making them perfect escape routes when cornered.

pegsolitaire
Peg Solitaire
The classic peg jumping puzzle! Jump over pegs to remove them. Goal: leave only one peg on the board. Simple rules, deep strategy!

Peg Solitaire is the centuries-old board puzzle that has challenged minds for generations! The board is filled with pegs except for one central hole. Jump pegs over each other to remove them, aiming to leave just one peg remaining. This seemingly simple goal requires careful planning and strategic thinking—the best players can finish with that single peg right in the center!

Remove pegs from the board by jumping over them with other pegs, similar to checkers. The ultimate goal is to finish with exactly one peg remaining, ideally in the central position. Plan each move carefully!
Click a peg to select it, then click an empty hole two spaces away to jump. The peg between your start and end positions is removed. Continue until no more jumps are possible.
Click on a peg to select it, then click on an empty hole exactly two spaces away in a straight line (horizontal or vertical). The peg you jumped over is automatically removed.
Jump over adjacent peg into empty space
Jumped peg is removed
Only horizontal/vertical jumps allowed
No diagonal jumps
Game ends when no jumps possible
Classic Layout - Traditional board
Strategic Depth - Complex planning
Perfect Game Goal - One peg remaining
Plan several moves ahead
Work from the edges toward the center
Avoid isolating pegs that can't be jumped
The perfect game leaves one peg in the center
What's the best final position?

The perfect game ends with one peg in the center of the board—the same position that was empty at the start. This is considered the 'standard' solution, though other single-peg finishes are also victories.
Is it always possible to win?
With the standard English board (33 holes), it IS possible to finish with one peg. However, achieving this requires careful planning. Many positions become unsolvable if you make wrong moves early.
Work systematically to avoid isolating pegs. Keep pegs clustered so they can jump each other. Many players work from edges toward center. Avoid creating 'islands' of pegs that can't reach each other.
How many moves does a perfect game take?
A perfect game on the standard board takes exactly 31 moves (removing 31 pegs, leaving 1). The minimum is fixed—you can't solve it in fewer moves because each move removes exactly one peg.

点击棋子选择它，然后点击两个空间外的空孔来跳跃。你起点和终点之间的棋子会被移除。继续直到没有更多跳跃可能。

最佳最终位置是什么？
完美的游戏在棋盘中心留下一个棋子——与开始时空的位置相同。这被认为是'标准'解决方案，尽管其他单棋子完成也是胜利。
总是可能赢吗？
在标准英式棋盘（33个孔）上，可以在一个棋子完成。然而，实现这一点需要仔细规划。如果你在早期做出错误的移动，许多位置会变得无法解决。
系统地工作以避免孤立棋子。保持棋子聚集以便它们可以互相跳跃。许多玩家从边缘向中心工作。避免创建无法互相到达的棋子'岛屿'。
完美的游戏需要多少步？
标准棋盘上的完美游戏需要正好31步（移除31个棋子，留下1个）。最小值是固定的——你不能在更少的步数内解决它，因为每步正好移除一个棋子。

Pong
The game that started it all

Pong is the granddaddy of all video games—the game that launched an industry! This 1972 Atari classic simulates a table tennis match with simple yet addictive gameplay. Two paddles, one ball, and endless competitive fun. Whether you remember playing this in arcades or are discovering it for the first time, Pong delivers pure, distilled gaming excitement that has stood the test of time for over 50 years.

Score points by getting the ball past your opponent's paddle. The first player to reach the target score wins. Master the angles, anticipate bounces, and outmaneuver the AI to become the Pong champion!
Use arrow keys or W/S to move your paddle up and down. Hit the ball to send it back. Score when the ball passes your opponent's paddle. Choose difficulty for AI challenge level.

How do I control the ball direction?
Hit the ball with the edge of your paddle to create sharper angles. The center creates straight shots while edges add spin and angle.
Why does the ball speed up?
Ball speed increases with each hit during a rally, making long rallies more challenging and exciting. Missing resets the speed.
What's the best strategy against hard AI?
Use angles to force the AI to move quickly. Try to wrong-foot it by hitting opposite corners. Stay centered when possible.
Can two players play?
This version features single-player against AI. Challenge yourself across multiple difficulty levels from beginner to expert!

Reaction Test
Test your reflexes and reaction speed

The Reaction Test is a classic way to measure your reflexes! Wait for the screen to turn from red to green, then click as fast as you can. Your reaction time is measured in milliseconds. The average human reaction time is about 250ms—can you beat that? Track your best times, identify your average, and see how you compare!
Click as quickly as possible when the screen turns green. Achieve the fastest reaction time possible. Track your progress across multiple attempts and try to improve your average response time.
Click Start, then wait for the screen to turn from red to green. Click immediately when you see green. Don't click too early or you'll have to restart.
Click anywhere on the screen or press any key when the screen turns green. Early clicks (before green) require a restart.

Early click = restart required
Track best time and average across attempts
Millisecond Precision - Accurate timing
Multiple Attempts - Establish your average
Simple Controls - Click or press any key
Stay relaxed - tension slows reactions
Focus on the center of the screen
Don't anticipate - wait for the actual signal
Practice regularly to improve your baseline
What's a good reaction time?
Average is about 250ms. Good is under 200ms. Excellent is under 150ms. Professional gamers often achieve 100-150ms. Under 100ms is exceptional!
Why do I get different times each attempt?
Human reaction time varies naturally due to focus, fatigue, and anticipation. Taking multiple attempts gives you a more accurate picture of your true reaction speed.
Can I improve my reaction time?
Yes! Regular practice, staying relaxed, good sleep, and avoiding distractions all help. However, there's a biological limit—most improvement comes from eliminating delays, not increasing raw speed.
What happens if I click too early?
The test resets and you must start over. This prevents cheating by randomly clicking. Waiting for the actual signal is part of the test!

什么是好的反应时间？
平均约250毫秒。好是200毫秒以下。优秀是150毫秒以下。职业玩家通常达到100-150毫秒。100毫秒以下是例外！
为什么每次尝试时间不同？
人类反应时间因注意力、疲劳和预期而自然变化。多次尝试可以更准确地反映你的真实反应速度。
我可以改善反应时间吗？
可以！定期练习、保持放松、良好的睡眠和避免干扰都有帮助。但是，存在生物限制——大多数改善来自消除延迟，而非增加原始速度。
如果我点击太早会怎样？
测试重置，你必须重新开始。这防止了随机点击作弊。等待真正信号是测试的一部分！

Reaction Time is the ultimate test of your reflexes! Wait for the visual signal to appear, then click as fast as you possibly can. Your reaction time is measured in milliseconds, giving you precise feedback on your reflex speed. Compare your times with friends, track your improvement, and see how you stack up against the average human reaction time of about 250ms!

What's considered a fast reaction time?
Average is around 250ms. Good is 200-220ms. Very good is 150-200ms. Excellent is under 150ms. Professional esports players often have times under 150ms.
Why do my times vary?
Human reaction time naturally varies based on alertness, focus, anticipation, and even time of day. Taking 5+ attempts gives a more accurate average.
Can training improve reaction time?
Yes! Regular practice, good sleep, and staying relaxed all help. Most improvement comes from eliminating wasted time rather than increasing raw neural speed.
How do professionals have such fast times?
Professional gamers develop muscle memory and learn to anticipate cues. They also maintain peak physical condition and eliminate all unnecessary movements.

Reversi
Flip discs to dominate the board

Reversi (also known as Othello) is a classic strategy board game of territory and capture! Place discs to outflank and flip your opponent's pieces to your color. The rules are simple—sandwich opponent discs between your own to capture them—but the strategy runs deep. At game's end, the player with more discs wins. Easy to learn, difficult to master, Reversi offers endless tactical challenge!
End the game with more discs of your color than your opponent. Maximize your disc count by planning moves that flip multiple opponent discs while protecting your own from counter-flips.
Click on a valid cell to place your disc. You must flip at least one opponent disc. Discs are flipped when sandwiched between your discs. Game ends when no moves remain.
Click on any highlighted valid move to place your disc. Valid moves must outflank at least one opponent disc. Outflanked discs are flipped to your color.
Black plays first, players alternate turns
A valid move must flip at least one opponent disc
Discs are flipped when sandwiched between your discs (in any direction)
If no valid move exists, turn passes to opponent
Game ends when neither player can move
Valid Move Highlighting - See where you can play
Disc Counter - Track who's winning
Pass Detection - Automatic turn passing
Control corners - they can never be flipped
Avoid spaces next to corners early in the game
Focus on stable discs that can't be flipped
Mobility matters - keep your move options open
What are corners so important?
Corner discs can never be flipped because they can't be outflanked. Controlling corners often leads to controlling the adjacent edges, creating stable positions.
Is having more discs early good?
Not necessarily! Early game disc count matters less than position. Sometimes having fewer discs means more mobility and better moves later.
What's a 'stable disc'?
A stable disc is one that can never be flipped regardless of future play. Corner discs are always stable, and discs adjacent to stable discs often become stable too.
When does the game end?
The game ends when neither player can make a valid move—usually when the board is full, but sometimes earlier if no flips are possible.
黑白棋
翻转棋子统治棋盘
黑白棋（又称奥赛罗）是一款关于领土和俘获的经典策略棋盘游戏！放置棋子夹击并将对手的棋子翻转为你的颜色。规则很简单——将对手棋子夹在你自己的棋子之间来俘获它们——但策略很深。游戏结束时棋子多的玩家获胜。易学难精，黑白棋提供无尽的战术挑战！
游戏结束时拥有比你对手更多颜色的棋子。通过规划翻转多个对手棋子的移动来最大化你的棋子数，同时保护自己的棋子不被反翻转。
点击有效位置放置棋子。你必须翻转至少一个对手棋子。棋子被夹在你的棋子之间时会被翻转。当没有可走时游戏结束。
点击任何高亮的有效位置放置棋子。有效移动必须夹击至少一个对手棋子。被夹击的棋子会翻转为你的颜色。

为什么角落很重要？
角落棋子永远不能被翻转，因为它们不能被夹击。控制角落通常导致控制相邻边缘，创造稳定位置。
早期拥有更多棋子好吗？
不一定！早期游戏棋子数量不如位置重要。有时拥有更少棋子意味着更多机动性和更好的后续移动。
什么是'稳定棋子'？
稳定棋子是无论后续游戏如何都永远不能被翻转的棋子。角落棋子总是稳定的，与稳定棋子相邻的棋子通常也会变得稳定。

Shisen-Sho
Match identical mahjong tiles to clear them! Tiles can only be matched if there's a clear path between them. A relaxing yet challenging puzzle game!

Shisen-Sho (also known as Sichuan Mahjong) is a tile-matching puzzle game using traditional mahjong tiles! Unlike other matching games, tiles don't need to be on the edges—they just need a clear connecting path with at most two turns. This creates strategic depth as you plan which pairs to remove first to unlock new matches. A perfect blend of relaxation and mental exercise!

Remove all tiles from the board by matching pairs of identical tiles. Tiles can only be matched if they can be connected by a path with no more than two turns (three line segments). Clear all tiles to win!
Click two matching tiles (same suit and value) to remove them. Tiles must be reachable without going through other tiles. Clear all tiles to win!

Traditional Chinese mahjong matching game
What counts as a valid path?

A valid path connects two tiles with at most two turns (three straight line segments). The path can go around other tiles but cannot pass through them. Think of it as connecting tiles with a pipe that can bend twice.
Can I match tiles that are blocked?
If a tile is completely surrounded by other tiles with no clear path out, it cannot be matched until some blocking tiles are removed. Plan your matches to unlock trapped tiles.
What's the difference from regular Mahjong Solitaire?

In Mahjong Solitaire, only free tiles (not covered or blocked on sides) can be matched. In Shisen-Sho, any visible tiles can be matched as long as there's a valid connecting path, even if they're in the middle of the board.
Are all puzzles solvable?
Most well-designed layouts are solvable, but some random arrangements may not be. If stuck with no possible matches, the game usually offers a shuffle or new game option.

什么算有效路径？
有效路径用最多两个转弯（三条直线段）连接两张牌。路径可以绕过其他牌但不能穿过它们。把它想象成用可以弯曲两次的管子连接牌。
我可以匹配被阻挡的牌吗？

这与普通麻将接龙有什么不同？
在麻将接龙中，只有自由的牌（没有被覆盖或侧面被阻挡）可以被匹配。在四川麻将中，任何可见的牌都可以被匹配，只要有有效的连接路径，即使它们在棋盘中间。

Simon Says
Follow the color and sound sequence! Each round gets longer and harder. A fun way to train your auditory and visual memory simultaneously.

Simon Says is the classic electronic memory game that has tested players since 1978! Watch and listen as the game plays a sequence of colored lights and tones. Then repeat the exact sequence back by pressing the colored buttons. Each round adds one more step to the pattern—how long can you keep up? This iconic game trains both visual and auditory memory simultaneously!

Watch and memorize the sequence of colors and sounds, then repeat the pattern exactly. Progress to increasingly longer sequences and track your personal best. Train your working memory and pattern recognition!

The average person can remember 7±2 items in sequence. With practice, many reach 15-20 steps. Memory champions can handle 30+ through chunking and mnemonic techniques.
What's the best way to remember long sequences?
Use chunking—group 3-4 colors into patterns. Create stories or associations (red-blue-yellow = 'fire-ocean-sun'). Say colors aloud to engage multiple memory systems.
Does Simon Says really improve memory?
Yes! Simon exercises working memory, pattern recognition, and attention. Regular play can improve your ability to hold and manipulate information in short-term memory.
Why is it called Simon Says?

The game is named after the children's game 'Simon Says' where players must follow commands. The electronic version was created by Ralph Baer and Howard Morrison in 1978, becoming one of the first electronic games.

Simon Game
Watch and repeat the color sequence

Simon is the classic electronic memory game that has challenged players since 1978! Watch as the colored buttons light up in a sequence, then repeat the pattern exactly. Each round adds one more color to remember, pushing your memory to its limits. This simple yet addictive game tests both visual and auditory memory, making it a perfect brain exercise for all ages. How long a sequence can you remember?

Remember and repeat increasingly long sequences of colors and sounds. Each successful round adds one more step to the sequence. Challenge yourself to beat your high score and expand your memory capacity.

Does the game speed up?
In many versions, yes! As sequences get longer, the playback speed may increase, adding another layer of challenge.
Is there a limit to how long sequences can get?
Theoretically, no! The record is over 100 steps. Human memory typically tops out around 20-30, but with practice you can push further.
Why is Simon good for your brain?
Simon exercises working memory, attention, and pattern recognition. Regular play can help improve short-term memory and focus.

Slitherlink
Draw a single continuous loop through the grid! Use number clues to determine which edges of each cell are part of the loop. A satisfying logic puzzle.

Slitherlink is the elegant loop-drawing puzzle that has captivated logic enthusiasts worldwide! Each cell contains a number 0-3 indicating how many of its edges are part of the loop. Your task: draw a single continuous loop that never crosses or branches. Using pure deduction, you'll discover which edges belong to the loop and which don't. Simple rules, deeply satisfying solutions!

Draw a single continuous loop through the grid edges. The loop must pass through exactly the number of edges shown by each clue (0, 1, 2, or 3). The loop cannot cross itself or branch. Find the unique solution through logical deduction!
Click edges to include them in the loop. Numbers 0-3 indicate how many edges around that cell are used. Create one continuous loop with no crossings or branches.
Click on the edges between dots to toggle them as part of the loop or not. Numbers inside cells tell you how many of that cell's four edges are part of the loop. Build the loop section by section.

0 means no edges around that cell

What does a 0 clue mean?
A cell with 0 means none of its four edges are part of the loop. Mark all four edges as 'not loop' immediately—this is crucial starting information!
How do I handle a 3 in a corner?
A 3 in a corner is powerful! The cell has only two edges on the corner, so both must be part of the loop. Plus, the loop must continue, which forces specific patterns.
What about diagonal 3s?
When two 3s are diagonal from each other, they create a specific pattern. The edges that would connect them diagonally must NOT be part of the loop. This is a key deduction rule.
How do I know the loop is correct?

The loop must be one continuous closed curve (like a rubber band). Every clue must be satisfied. There should be no loose ends, branches, or crossings. Following the loop should return you to where you started.

蛇会加速吗？
许多版本随着蛇变长而增加速度，使游戏逐渐更具挑战性。这增加了提前规划而非反应的压力。

Solitaire TriPeaks
Clear three peaks by playing sequential cards

Solitaire TriPeaks is an addictive card game that combines strategy with luck! Three pyramids of cards await—you must clear them by playing cards that are one rank higher or lower than the current waste pile card. Unlike classic solitaire, suit doesn't matter here! Plan your moves to uncover buried cards, create long chains for bonus points, and clear all three peaks to win!

Remove all cards from the three pyramid peaks by playing sequentially—each card played must be one rank higher or lower than the top waste card. Clear the entire board before running out of stock cards!

Can I play cards of any suit?
Yes! In TriPeaks, suit doesn't matter. You can play any card that's one rank higher or lower than the waste pile, regardless of suit color or symbol.
What happens when I run out of moves?
Click the stock pile to draw a new card. If the stock runs out, you can cycle through it—but recycling usually costs points!
Should I clear one peak first or balance them?
Generally, try to keep options open by playing from multiple peaks. However, clearing one peak early can be strategic if it unlocks many buried cards.
What's a streak bonus?
Playing consecutive cards without drawing from stock builds a streak. Longer streaks multiply your points—plan ahead to maximize chains!

Solitaire
The classic Klondike card game

Solitaire (Klondike) is the world's most popular card game, beloved by millions since its inclusion in Microsoft Windows! This single-player card game challenges you to arrange a standard 52-card deck into four foundation piles, sorted by suit from Ace to King. Simple to learn yet deeply strategic, Solitaire offers the perfect balance of relaxation and mental stimulation. Whether you have five minutes or an hour, this timeless classic provides endless entertainment.

Move all 52 cards to four foundation piles, sorted by suit from Ace to King. Build tableau columns in descending order with alternating colors. Expose hidden cards and use strategy to uncover the entire deck.

Drag cards to move them. Build tableau columns in descending order with alternating colors (red on black, black on red). Move cards to foundation piles in ascending order by suit (A to K). Click the stock pile to draw new cards.

Is every Solitaire game winnable?
No, not every game of Solitaire is winnable. It's estimated that 79-80% of games are theoretically winnable, but finding the solution requires perfect play.
Should I move cards to foundations immediately?
Not always. Sometimes keeping cards in the tableau gives you more flexibility. Move to foundations when it doesn't block potential moves.
What's the best strategy for empty columns?
Only place Kings in empty columns when it helps expose face-down cards. A King with a long stack behind it is more valuable than one alone.
How do I know when to draw from stock?
Draw when no more productive moves are available in the tableau. But scan carefully first - sometimes hidden moves exist!

How can I improve my mental math speed?
Practice regularly, learn mental math shortcuts (like multiplying by 10 and adjusting), break complex problems into smaller steps, and focus on accuracy before speed.
Maintain accuracy first—a long streak with a multiplier is worth more than fast but wrong answers. Stay calm and don't rush into mistakes.
Are there tricks for specific operations?
Yes! For multiplication, break numbers into factors. For division, estimate first. For addition/subtraction, round to easy numbers then adjust. Practice makes these automatic.
Does this game really help with math skills?
Absolutely! Regular mental math practice improves number sense, working memory, and calculation speed. These skills transfer to real-world math and academic performance.

What is a pangram?
A pangram is a word that uses all 7 available letters at least once. Finding the pangram gives you a big score boost and is often key to reaching higher ranks.
Can I use letters more than once?
Yes! You can use any letter multiple times in a word. For example, if you have 'L', you can spell 'LEVEL'.
How do I reach Queen Bee status?
Queen Bee requires finding all possible words in the puzzle. It's extremely difficult and often requires hours of play or collaborative effort.
What's the shuffle button for?
Shuffling rearranges the 6 outer letters randomly. This can help you see new letter combinations and break out of word-finding ruts.

Star Battle
Place stars without any touching

Star Battle is a spatial logic puzzle that challenges you to place stars strategically! Each row, column, and colored region must contain exactly two stars—but here's the catch: no two stars can ever touch, not even diagonally. This creates a fascinating interplay of constraints that requires careful deduction and planning. A perfect blend of sudoku-style logic and spatial reasoning!
Place exactly two stars in each row, column, and colored region while ensuring no stars touch each other (including diagonally). Use logical deduction to find the unique valid arrangement of stars.

Can stars touch diagonally?
No! Stars cannot touch in any direction, including diagonally. This is what makes Star Battle challenging—you must leave at least one empty cell between any two stars.
Where should I start?
Start with the smallest regions—they have the most constraints and often force star positions. Also look for rows or columns that are nearly complete.
Click on a placed star to remove it. Use the process of elimination—when a cell must be empty due to nearby stars, it helps narrow down where stars can go.
Is guessing required?
No. Like all good logic puzzles, Star Battle can be solved entirely through logical deduction. If you're guessing, you may have missed a constraint.

stroop-test
strooptest
Stroop Test
Test cognitive flexibility and control

The Stroop Test is a famous psychological experiment that measures your brain's processing speed and cognitive control! You'll see color words displayed in various ink colors. Your task: determine if the ink color matches the word's meaning. The catch? Your brain automatically reads the word, creating interference when they don't match. This classic test reveals fascinating insights about how your mind works!

Correctly identify whether the text color matches the word meaning as quickly as possible. Achieve high accuracy while maintaining speed. This test measures your cognitive flexibility and ability to suppress automatic responses.
You'll see a word displayed in a certain color. Click 'MATCH' if the color matches the word meaning, or 'NO MATCH' if they differ. Answer as quickly and accurately as possible!
You'll see a color word (like 'RED') displayed in a certain ink color. Click MATCH if the ink color matches what the word says, or NO MATCH if they differ. React quickly!
Words are color names (RED, BLUE, GREEN, YELLOW)
Text can appear in matching or different colors
Your brain wants to read the word, not see the color
This creates interference when they don't match
Score based on both speed and accuracy
Speed Measurement - Track reaction time
Accuracy Tracking - Monitor correct responses
Progressive Difficulty - More challenging patterns
Instant Feedback - Know if you're right or wrong
Focus on the text color, not the word meaning
The instinct to read the word can interfere
Stay calm and concentrate
Accuracy matters as much as speed
What is the Stroop effect?
The Stroop effect is a psychological phenomenon where reading a word interferes with naming the color it's printed in. It demonstrates how automatic reading interferes with color naming.
Why is this test hard?
Reading has become automatic for most adults. Your brain reads the word before you can process the ink color, creating interference when they don't match.
What does a good score mean?
Fast, accurate responses indicate strong cognitive control and processing speed. The ability to quickly suppress the automatic reading response is a sign of good executive function.
Can I improve at this?
Yes! Practice helps your brain get better at focusing on color rather than meaning. However, the Stroop effect is robust—it never completely goes away.

测试认知灵活性和控制力
斯特鲁普测试是一项著名的心理学实验，测量你大脑的处理速度和认知控制！你会看到用各种墨水颜色显示的颜色词。你的任务：判断墨水颜色是否与词的含义匹配。关键在于？你的大脑会自动阅读单词，当它们不匹配时会产生干扰。这个经典测试揭示了关于你大脑如何运作的有趣见解！
尽可能快地正确识别文字颜色是否与单词含义匹配。在保持速度的同时实现高准确率。这个测试测量你的认知灵活性和抑制自动反应的能力。
你会看到一个用特定颜色显示的单词。如果颜色与单词含义匹配，点击'MATCH'，如果不匹配点击'NO MATCH'。尽可能快速准确地回答！
你会看到一个颜色词（如'RED'）以某种墨水颜色显示。如果墨水颜色与单词所说的一致点击MATCH，如果不同点击NO MATCH。快速反应！

什么是斯特鲁普效应？
斯特鲁普效应是一种心理现象，阅读单词会干扰说出它印刷的颜色。它展示了自动阅读如何干扰颜色命名。
为什么这个测试很难？
阅读对大多数成年人来说已经变得自动。你的大脑在你能处理墨水颜色之前就读了单词，当它们不匹配时产生干扰。
好分数意味着什么？
快速、准确的响应表明强大的认知控制和处理速度。快速抑制自动阅读反应的能力是良好执行功能的标志。
我可以在这方面提高吗？
可以！练习帮助你的大脑更好地专注于颜色而非含义。然而，斯特鲁普效应是稳健的——它永远不会完全消失。

Sudoku
Fill a 9×9 grid with digits 1-9

Sudoku is a legendary number puzzle game that has captivated millions of players worldwide. Your goal is to fill a 9×9 grid with digits 1-9, ensuring each row, column, and 3×3 box contains every number exactly once. With simple rules but infinite complexity, Sudoku offers the perfect balance of challenge and satisfaction for puzzle enthusiasts of all skill levels. Whether you're a beginner looking for a relaxing brain exercise or an expert seeking the ultimate mental challenge, Sudoku delivers endless hours of logical entertainment.

Complete the grid by placing numbers 1-9 in every row, column, and 3×3 box without repetition. Challenge yourself to solve puzzles faster and with fewer mistakes. Track your progress across multiple difficulty levels from Easy to Expert.

Is there always only one solution?
Yes, every valid Sudoku puzzle has exactly one unique solution that can be found through logic alone, without guessing.
Start by scanning rows, columns, and boxes for numbers that can only go in one place. Use the notes feature liberally to track possibilities.
How do I get better at Sudoku?
Practice regularly, learn advanced techniques like naked pairs and hidden singles, and challenge yourself with harder difficulty levels.
What makes a Sudoku hard?
Harder puzzles require more advanced solving techniques and have fewer initial clues, requiring deeper logical reasoning chains.

Tangram
Classic Chinese dissection puzzle

Tangram (七巧板) is the ancient Chinese puzzle that has challenged minds for over 200 years! Using just 7 geometric pieces (called tans), create thousands of different shapes and figures. The rules are simple: use all 7 pieces to form the target silhouette without overlapping. But the possibilities are endless! From animals to buildings to abstract art, Tangram exercises your spatial reasoning and creativity in unique ways.

Arrange all 7 tangram pieces to match the target silhouette. Rotate and position each piece carefully—no pieces may overlap and all must be used. Complete various challenges from simple shapes to complex figures!

What are the 7 tangram pieces?
Two large triangles, one medium triangle, two small triangles, one square, and one parallelogram. Together they form a square when arranged correctly.
Why is the parallelogram special?
The parallelogram is the only piece that may need to be flipped (not just rotated) to fit certain puzzles. This adds extra challenge!
How many puzzles are possible?
In theory, thousands of different silhouettes can be created! Traditional sets include animals, people, buildings, and abstract shapes.
What skills does tangram develop?
Tangram develops spatial reasoning, geometric understanding, problem-solving, and creativity. It's excellent for both children learning shapes and adults keeping their minds sharp.

7块七巧板是什么？
两个大三角形、一个中三角形、两个小三角形、一个正方形和一个平行四边形。正确排列时它们组成一个正方形。

七巧板培养空间推理、几何理解、问题解决和创造力。它非常适合儿童学习形状和成人保持头脑敏锐。

Tetris
Rotate and place falling blocks to clear lines

Tetris is the legendary puzzle game that has defined the genre for over four decades! Guide falling tetrominos (geometric shapes made of four blocks) as they descend, rotating and positioning them to create complete horizontal lines. When a line is complete, it disappears, scoring points and making room for more pieces. As the game progresses, the pieces fall faster, testing your reflexes, spatial reasoning, and strategic planning. From its Soviet origins in 1984 to its status as a global cultural phenomenon, Tetris remains the gold standard of puzzle gaming.

Survive as long as possible by clearing horizontal lines to prevent the stack from reaching the top. Score points by clearing multiple lines at once—especially the coveted four-line 'Tetris'. Challenge yourself to beat your high score and improve your speed and accuracy.
Use arrow keys to move and rotate falling blocks. Left/Right to move, Up to rotate, Down to soft drop. Complete horizontal lines are cleared. The game speeds up as you progress.

通过消除水平线尽可能长时间地生存，防止方块堆到顶部。一次消除多行可以获得更多分数——尤其是令人向往的四行'俄罗斯方块'。挑战自己打破最高分，提高速度和准确性。

Text Twist
Rearrange jumbled letters to form as many words as possible! Race against the clock to find all valid words before time runs out.

Text Twist is the fast-paced word game that challenges your vocabulary under pressure! Given six scrambled letters, you must form as many valid words as possible before the timer runs out. Find at least one 6-letter word to advance to the next round. The combination of time pressure and word-finding makes this an addictive brain workout for language lovers!

Form as many words as possible from the given letters within the time limit. You must find at least one word using all letters (typically 6 letters) to advance to the next round. Score points based on word length and clear all words to get bonus points!
Click letters to add them to your word, then click Submit to check if it's valid. You can also click Enter to submit. Find as many words as you can within 2 minutes!

How do I advance to the next round?
You must find at least one word that uses all six letters. Without the 6-letter word, you cannot advance even if you found all other words. Prioritize finding the full-length word!
What's the minimum word length?
Words must be at least 3 letters long. Two-letter combinations don't count as valid words in Text Twist, so focus on finding 3, 4, 5, and 6 letter words.
Should I use the shuffle button?
Yes! Shuffling rearranges the letters visually, which can help you see combinations you missed. It's free to use and often reveals new word possibilities.
What happens if I find all words?
Congratulations! You'll receive bonus points and advance to the next round. This is the ideal outcome, though it requires extensive vocabulary and quick thinking.

Threes
The original sliding number puzzle! Slide tiles to combine matching numbers. 1+2=3, then combine 3s into 6s, 6s into 12s, and beyond. Addictive and strategic!

Threes is the award-winning puzzle game that inspired the entire 2048 genre! Slide numbered tiles on a grid to combine them—1+2 makes 3, and then matching numbers merge (3+3=6, 6+6=12, etc.). Unlike simple addition games, Threes requires careful planning because 1s and 2s don't combine with themselves. This elegant mechanic creates deep strategic gameplay that's easy to learn but challenging to master!

Slide tiles to combine numbers and create the highest possible value. Combine 1 and 2 to make 3, then merge matching numbers (3+3, 6+6, 12+12, etc.) to grow your tiles. Achieve the highest score before the board fills up!
Swipe to slide all tiles. Combine 1 and 2 to make 3. Match same numbers (3+3, 6+6, etc.) to merge. New tiles appear after each move.
Swipe or use arrow keys to slide all tiles in that direction. Tiles slide as far as possible until they hit another tile or the wall. Merges happen when compatible tiles collide.
1 + 2 = 3 (only way to create 3s)
Matching numbers merge (3+3, 6+6, etc.)
New tiles appear after each move
Game ends when board is full
Score based on tile values
Unique Merging - 1+2=3 system
Next Tile Preview - Plan ahead
Strategic Depth - Complex decisions
High Score Challenge - Beat your best
Always keep 1s and 2s adjacent to combine them
Build high numbers in corners
Plan moves to avoid trapping small numbers
The next tile preview helps you plan
How is Threes different from 2048?
Threes uses a unique 1+2=3 merging system, while 2048 allows any matching numbers to combine. Threes also shows the next tile preview and has more strategic depth due to the 1/2 mechanic.
What's the highest possible tile?
In theory, there's no limit! Players have reached tiles in the millions. However, achieving extremely high tiles requires perfect play and significant luck with tile placement.
Should I prioritize combining 1s and 2s?
Yes! Unlike other numbers, 1s and 2s can ONLY combine with each other. If they get trapped or separated, you lose the ability to create new 3s. Always keep them accessible.
Keep your highest tile in a corner and build around it. Keep 1s and 2s adjacent to each other. Use the next tile preview to plan. Avoid filling the board by always leaving merge opportunities.

三消是启发整个2048流派的获奖益智游戏！在网格上滑动编号的方块来合并它们——1+2变成3，然后匹配的数字合并（3+3=6, 6+6=12等）。与简单的加法游戏不同，三消需要仔细规划，因为1和2不能与自己结合。这个优雅的机制创造了深刻的战略游戏玩法，易学但难精！

Tic-Tac-Toe
The simplest yet timeless game! Get three Xs or Os in a row to win. Play against a friend or the AI. Perfect for quick strategic thinking!

Tic-Tac-Toe is the classic paper-and-pencil game that has taught generations about strategy! Two players take turns marking X and O on a 3×3 grid, racing to get three in a row. Despite its simple rules, the game contains deep strategic elements—forks, blocks, and optimal play patterns. It's the perfect introduction to competitive strategy games and a great way to practice tactical thinking!

Be the first player to place three of your marks (X or O) in a horizontal, vertical, or diagonal row on the 3×3 grid. Block your opponent's winning attempts while setting up your own victory. Master the optimal strategies to never lose!
Players take turns placing X or O in empty cells. First to get three in a row (horizontally, vertically, or diagonally) wins. If all cells fill with no winner, it's a draw.
Click on any empty cell to place your mark. X always goes first. The game alternates between players until someone wins or the grid is full.
3×3 grid with 9 cells
X always makes the first move
Three in a row wins the game
Full grid with no winner is a draw
Perfect play leads to draw
Two-Player Mode - Play with a friend
Quick Games - Under a minute each
Strategy Practice - Learn optimal play
Always take the center if available
Block opponent's winning moves immediately
Create "forks" - two winning threats at once
Corners are more valuable than edges
The center is the strongest opening because it's part of 4 winning lines. If the center is taken, any corner is equally good. Edges are the weakest opening moves.
Can Tic-Tac-Toe always be won?
With perfect play from both players, Tic-Tac-Toe always ends in a draw. However, most casual players make mistakes that can be exploited!
What is a fork in Tic-Tac-Toe?
A fork is when you create two winning threats simultaneously. Since your opponent can only block one, you're guaranteed to win on your next move.
How many possible games of Tic-Tac-Toe exist?
There are 255,168 possible games of Tic-Tac-Toe, but only 26,830 unique board positions when accounting for rotations and reflections.

Typing Test
Test and improve your typing speed

Typing Test is the ultimate way to measure and improve your keyboard skills! Type displayed words as quickly and accurately as possible while your Words Per Minute (WPM) and accuracy are tracked in real-time. Whether you're a beginner learning touch typing or an expert aiming to break 100 WPM, this test provides valuable feedback to help you improve. Perfect for students, professionals, and anyone who wants to type faster!

Type the displayed words as quickly and accurately as possible. Achieve the highest WPM (Words Per Minute) while maintaining accuracy above 95%. Track your progress over time and challenge yourself to improve.
Words appear on screen - type them exactly as shown. Press space after each word to move to the next. Your WPM and accuracy are tracked in real-time.

What is a good typing speed?
Average typing speed is 40 WPM. 60+ WPM is good, 80+ is excellent, and 100+ is professional level. Focus on accuracy first!
How can I type faster?
Learn proper finger placement (home row), practice regularly, and focus on accuracy. Speed naturally increases with muscle memory.
Why is accuracy important?
High speed with low accuracy means lots of corrections, which actually slows you down. Aim for 95%+ accuracy before focusing on speed.
What's touch typing?
Touch typing means typing without looking at the keyboard. Your fingers stay on the home row (ASDF JKL;) and reach for other keys. It's the fastest method!

Yajilin
Draw a loop and shade cells! Arrows indicate how many shaded cells are in that direction. Every cell must be either part of the loop or shaded.

Yajilin is the clever hybrid puzzle that combines cell-shading with loop-drawing! Numbered arrows point in a direction and tell you how many black cells exist in that direction. Your task: shade some cells black, then draw a single continuous loop through all remaining non-clue cells. Black cells can never touch, and the loop must visit every white cell. A perfect blend of two puzzle types!

Shade some cells black (following arrow clues), then draw a single continuous loop through all remaining non-clue cells. Black cells cannot touch orthogonally. The loop cannot cross or branch. Every non-clue cell must be either black or part of the loop!
Shade some cells black so that no two black cells touch. Draw a single loop through all non-shaded, non-clue cells. Clues show how many black cells are in the indicated direction.

Click cells to toggle them between white, black, or part of the loop. Arrow clues indicate how many black cells are in that direction from the clue. All non-clue cells must be either black or part of the loop.
Arrows point to black cell count
Loop passes through all white cells
Clue cells are not part of loop or black
Hybrid Puzzle - Two mechanics combined
Arrow Clues - Directional hints
Loop Drawing - Continuous path
Shading Logic - Strategic placement
Start with clue cells - they tell you where black cells must be
Black cells cannot touch each other orthogonally
The loop must visit every white cell exactly once
Use process of elimination for cells that cannot be black
Look at arrow clues first. A clue pointing at only a few cells forces specific cells to be black (or not black if the count is 0). This gives you anchor points to work outward from.
How do I know if a cell must be part of the loop?
If a cell cannot be black (because it would touch another black cell, or violate arrow counts), and it's not a clue cell, it must be part of the loop. Process of elimination is key.
What if the loop gets stuck?
The loop must visit every white cell and cannot branch. If you create a dead end, one of your assumptions is wrong. Backtrack and consider which black cell placements might be incorrect.
Can the loop pass through clue cells?
No! Clue cells are neither black nor part of the loop—they're neutral. The loop only passes through regular white cells. This is important for counting available loop cells.

耶吉林是将单元格涂黑与画环结合的聪明混合谜题！带编号的箭头指向一个方向，告诉你那个方向有多少黑色单元格。你的任务：将一些单元格涂黑，然后画一条通过所有剩余非提示单元格的单一连续回路。黑色单元格永远不能接触，回路必须访问每个白色单元格。两种谜题类型的完美结合！

点击单元格在白色、黑色或回路的一部分之间切换。箭头提示指示从提示那个方向有多少黑色单元格。所有非提示单元格必须是黑色或回路的一部分。

首先看箭头提示。指向只有几个单元格的提示强迫特定单元格是黑色（或者如果计数是0则不是黑色）。这给你向外工作的锚点。
我怎么知道单元格是否必须是回路的一部分？
如果单元格不能是黑色（因为它会接触另一个黑色单元格，或违反箭头计数），而且它不是提示单元格，它必须是回路的一部分。消除法是关键。
如果回路卡住了怎么办？
回路必须访问每个白色单元格，不能分支。如果你创建了死胡同，你的假设之一是错误的。回溯并考虑哪些黑色单元格放置可能是不正确的。
回路可以通过提示单元格吗？
不可以！提示单元格既不是黑色也不是回路的一部分——它们是中性的。回路只通过普通白色单元格。这对于计数可用回路单元格很重要。

What directions can words be hidden?
Words can be placed in eight directions: left to right, right to left, top to bottom, bottom to top, and all four diagonal directions. Always check every direction!
Can letters be used in multiple words?
Yes! A single letter can be part of multiple words. Just because you found one word using a letter doesn't mean there isn't another word using that same letter.
What's the best scanning strategy?
Start by looking for unusual letters (Q, X, Z, J) since they're easier to spot. Then scan for the first letter of each word systematically, checking all directions from that letter.
Does word search improve brain function?
Yes! Word searches help improve visual scanning, pattern recognition, vocabulary, and spelling. They're also great for maintaining cognitive function and can be relaxing stress relievers.

Race against time to unscramble words! Study the jumbled letters and type the correct word. Fast-paced vocabulary challenge!

Word Scramble is the fast-paced word game that tests your vocabulary and pattern recognition! A jumbled collection of letters appears on screen, and you must quickly identify the correct word they spell. With the clock ticking, your brain races through possible combinations—training both your language skills and quick thinking. The more you play, the sharper your word recognition becomes!

Unscramble the jumbled letters to form the correct word before time runs out. Type your answer as quickly as possible to earn maximum points. Build your vocabulary and improve your anagram-solving speed!

Hint System - Reveal letters
Look for common letter patterns like TH, CH, ING

Start with what you know and work from there
What's the best strategy for solving scrambles?
Look for common prefixes (un-, re-, pre-) and suffixes (-ing, -tion, -ly) first. Identify vowels and try placing consonants around them. Saying combinations aloud often triggers recognition.
How can I improve at Word Scramble?
Build your vocabulary through reading, practice anagram exercises, and learn to recognize common letter patterns. The more words you know, the faster you'll spot them in scrambled form.
Are there tricks to unscrambling?

Yes! Look for common letter combinations (QU, TH, CH), identify the first letter (often easier to spot), and try rearranging letters in a circle visually. Practice develops intuition for letter patterns.
Does this help with vocabulary?
Absolutely! You'll encounter new words and reinforce ones you know. The active process of unscrambling creates stronger memory connections than passive reading.

在时间用尽之前将打乱的字母重组为正确的单词。尽可能快地输入你的答案以获得最高分。建立你的词汇量并提高你的变位词解决速度！

解决重组的最佳策略是什么？
首先寻找常见的前缀（un-, re-, pre-）和后缀（-ing, -tion, -ly）。识别元音并尝试将辅音放在它们周围。大声说出组合通常会触发识别。
如何在单词重组中进步？
通过阅读建立你的词汇量，练习变位词练习，并学会识别常见的字母模式。你认识的单词越多，你就越快能在打乱的形式中发现它们。
有重组的技巧吗？
是的！寻找常见的字母组合（QU, TH, CH），识别第一个字母（通常更容易发现），并尝试在视觉上将字母排列成圆圈。练习培养对字母模式的直觉。
这对词汇有帮助吗？
绝对有！你会遇到新单词并加强你知道的单词。重组的主动过程比被动阅读创造更强的记忆连接。
